chapter_context.summary: Ahab desires Naboth's vineyard, and Jezebel schemes Naboth's execution under false blasphemy charges; Elijah pronounces the violent deaths of Ahab and Jezebel; Ahab humbles himself before God.

outline.text: Naboth Refuses to Sell His Vineyard to Ahab
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Naboth Refuses to Sell His Vineyard to Ahab (21:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Jezebel Schemes Naboth's Death by False Accusation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jezebel Schemes Naboth's Death by False Accusation (21:5-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elijah Pronounces Doom on Ahab and Jezebel in Naboth's Vineyard
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elijah Pronounces Doom on Ahab and Jezebel in Naboth's Vineyard (21:17-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Ahab's Extreme Wickedness; His Sincere Humiliation and Postponed Judgment
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahab's Extreme Wickedness; His Sincere Humiliation and Postponed Judgment (21:25-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 21, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
